description: Lightweight insecure-code patterns for the code_patterns static module. Each rule is a regex matched per line against files with the listed suffixes. These are heuristics (a linter, not a prover): default to 'tentative' confidence where false positives are likely.

patterns.name: Use of eval()
patterns.description: eval() executes arbitrary Python and is dangerous on any untrusted input.
patterns.remediation: Avoid eval(); use ast.literal_eval() for data or an explicit parser.

patterns.name: Use of exec()
patterns.description: exec() runs arbitrary Python; risky with any externally-influenced input.
patterns.remediation: Refactor to avoid dynamic code execution.

patterns.name: subprocess with shell=True
patterns.description: shell=True passes a command through the shell, enabling command injection if any argument is attacker-influenced.
patterns.remediation: Pass arguments as a list and keep shell=False; never interpolate untrusted input into a shell string.

patterns.name: os.system()
patterns.description: os.system() invokes the shell and is prone to command injection.
patterns.remediation: Use subprocess.run([...]) with an argument list.

patterns.name: yaml.load without SafeLoader
patterns.description: yaml.load() with the default loader can construct arbitrary Python objects (code execution).

patterns.name: pickle.loads()
patterns.description: Unpickling untrusted data can execute arbitrary code.
patterns.remediation: Never unpickle untrusted data; use JSON or a safe schema-based format.

patterns.name: TLS verification disabled (verify=False)
patterns.description: Disabling certificate verification exposes the connection to man-in-the-middle attacks.
patterns.remediation: Remove verify=False; trust a proper CA bundle.

patterns.name: Weak hash (md5/sha1)
patterns.description: MD5/SHA-1 are unsuitable for security (signatures, password hashing, integrity).
patterns.remediation: Use SHA-256+; for passwords use bcrypt/scrypt/argon2.

patterns.name: Flask debug mode enabled
patterns.description: Running Flask/Werkzeug with debug=True exposes an interactive debugger that can execute code.
patterns.remediation: Never enable debug mode in production.

patterns.name: Insecure tempfile.mktemp()
patterns.description: mktemp() is subject to race conditions.

patterns.name: Use of eval()
patterns.remediation: Avoid eval(); use JSON.parse() for data.

patterns.name: new Function()
patterns.description: new Function() compiles strings into code, similar risk to eval().

patterns.name: child_process.exec()
patterns.description: exec() runs a command through the shell, enabling command injection.
patterns.remediation: Use execFile()/spawn() with an argument array.

patterns.name: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML
patterns.description: Injecting raw HTML can introduce XSS if the content is not sanitised.
patterns.remediation: Avoid raw HTML injection or sanitise with a vetted library (e.g. DOMPurify).

patterns.name: TLS verification disabled (rejectUnauthorized: false)
patterns.description: Disables TLS certificate validation, allowing man-in-the-middle attacks.
patterns.remediation: Remove rejectUnauthorized:false and trust a proper CA bundle.

patterns.name: NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED disabled
patterns.description: Globally disables Node.js TLS certificate validation.
